observations.recommendation:
Recommendations:
We hereby recommended the following:
- Existing practice of the agency on processing claims not duly supported with
documents shall be discontinued as this is not the proper way of processing
claims and it may further create adverse impact to agency and to concerned
officials if this will continue to exist in their transactions.
- Submit a written justification explaining therein the reasons for processing
claims without complete documentation.
- The Municipal Accountant shall likewise submit all the required
documentation needed to establish the validity and legality of the
disbursements and henceforth, payment shall only be made for claims
supported with complete documentation.

Recommendations:
We hereby recommended the following:
- The Municipal Treasurer shall render his monthly reports such as
Disbursement Vouchers (DVs) with supporting documents, Payrolls,
Official Receipts and other liquidation documents including revenue reports
and supporting documents to the Municipal Accountant on or before the 5th
day of the ensuing month to allow its prompt recording in the books of
accounts.
- The Municipal Accountant shall coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer to
ensure prompt submission of DVs and other related reports, undertake
recording in the books of accounts, and submit the same within 10 days of
the ensuing month to the Office of the Auditor.
- Concerned officials shall prioritize and adopt measures to address issues on
delayed submission of reports to be able to meet the deadline set and be free
from possible sanctions may be imposed from violating thereof as required
under article 218 of the Revised Penal Code.
- Require concerned official/s to submit written justification explaining
therein the reasons for late submission, and if not satisfied, appropriate
actions shall be instituted against the accountable officers for the
delayed/non submission of financial reports as required under Article 218
of the Revised Penal Code.

Recommendations:
We hereby recommended to management the following:
- To faithfully comply and enforce the periodic submission of these reports
within the prescribed period. Concerned officials shall likewise prioritize
submission of these reports and look into the root cause of these delays and
immediately initiate measures to address the problem.
- Require concerned official/s to submit written justification explaining
therein the reasons of late submission, and if not satisfied, appropriate
actions shall be instituted against the accountable officers for the delayed
submission of financial reports as required under Article 218 of the Revised
Penal Code.

Recommendations:
We hereby recommended the following:
- The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer shall exert
more efforts to maintain their respective accounting and budget records,
using the prescribed form, and update the same regularly.
- The necessity of additional workforce, who has the required expertise and
qualification, for the said offices shall be properly considered and shall be
prioritized as well including their attendance to trainings/seminars to keep
them abreast of the latest accounting and budget issuances.

Recommendations:
We hereby recommended the following:
- Intensify efforts for the timely conduct of annual physical count of PPE and
submit the required Reports on PPE to COA within deadline set.
- Invite representative from COA to witness the said inventory taking.
Invitation letter shall be sent three (3) days prior to the scheduled date to
give ample time to prepare and settle whatever time inconveniences that may
have.
- Prioritize the update of the property and accounting records including PPE
subsidiary ledger, and immediately present the same to the office of the
auditor for verification and review.

Recommendations:
We recommended to management the following:
- Submit all other transaction documents and so we could complete our audit
on GAD Funds and refrain from processing GAD transaction not duly
supported with documents.
- Immediately submit all other requirements in settlement of audit suspension
to avoid disallowance in audit; and it will facilitate completion of the audit on
GAD fund as well.

Recommendations:
The management shall undertake the following:
- Immediately submit all the lacking requirements in settlement of the audit
suspension to avoid disallowance in audit; and facilitate completion of the
audit on LDRRM fund as well.
- The unutilized balance of the LDRRM shall be immediately transferred to
the LDRRM special trust fund account and provide our office with the
necessary document to evidence the said fund transfer.

Recommendations:
We hereby recommended the following:
- The management shall faithfully follow correct disbursement system and so
only valid and proper claims will be processed and paid.
- Exert all possible efforts to settle all audit suspensions within the required
timeframe to avoid disallowance in audit, and submit as well all other related
transaction documents to enable us complete our audit on the said fund and
undertake the necessary validation and inspection.

The Municipal Accountant shall not process claims/payments with required documents. She shall likewise follow correct disbursement system at all times. Exert all possible efforts to settle all audit suspensions within the required timeframe to avoid disallowance in audit. Be guided of the pertinent sections in the afore-mentioned regulations and in other GPPB issued resolutions simplifying the requirements in project implementation during emergency cases.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendations:
The management shall undertake the following:
- The Municipal Accountant shall not process claims/payments not duly
supported with required documents. She shall likewise follow correct
disbursement system at all times.
- Exert all possible efforts to settle all audit suspensions within the required
timeframe to avoid disallowance in audit.
- Be guided of the pertinent sections in the afore-mentioned regulations and in
other GPPB issued resolutions simplifying the requirements in project
implementation during emergency cases.

Recommendation:
The Municipal Accountant shall prioritize submission of the COVID related
reports and transactions documents since the prescribed period within which to
submit has already lapsed, and the necessity of its immediate audit has been
laid down to establish proper utilization thereof.

Recommendations:
We hereby recommended the following:
- The Accounting, Supply and Property Units shall update regularly, PPE
Subsidiary Ledgers, Stock Cards and Property Cards, respectively, and
reconcile balances of these records with the Report on Physical Count of
Inventories and PPE to be able to reflect a complete and accurate balances in
the financial statements.
- The management shall likewise include in their yearly plan the conduct of the
required physical count of Inventories and PPE to be undertaken by the
agency’s Inventory Committee, and so required reports can be prepared and
submitted to COA on or before January 31 of the ensuing year.
- Invite representative from the Commission on Audit (COA) to act as witness
in every inventory-taking to comply with the regulation. Invitation letter shall be
served and stamped received by COA at least three days prior to the scheduled
date to give the auditor an ample time to prepare and settle whatever time
inconveniences that may have.

Recommendations:
We recommended the following:
- Prioritize submission of the monthly report of transactions to COA within
the deadline set to avoid negative repercussion; and timely audit of its
transactions could be undertaken as well.
- Maximize work assignment among accounting personnel and/or assign
additional staff, if the present workforce is not enough, so as to prevent work
overload and meet deadlines on reports submission.

Recommendations:
The management shall undertake the following:
- Exert all possible efforts to collate all the lacking documents and/or prepare
a written justification for the non-settlement of the audit suspensions, and
submit the same to COA for the necessary evaluation and review, otherwise,
the transactions covered by it will be disallowed in audit.
- Refrain from undertaking a project not supported with complete bid and
technical documentation. However, awarding of contract shall only be made
to a legally, financially and technically capable supplier/contractor and
willing to cooperate in the submission of required documents.
- Assign additional qualified personnel to take charge in the preparation of
documents and settlement of all audit suspensions to be able to meet the
required deadlines.

Recommendations:
We hereby recommended the following:
- The management shall faithfully follow correct disbursement system and so
only valid and proper claims will be processed and paid.
- Exert all possible efforts to settle all audit suspensions within the required
timeframe to avoid disallowance in audit, and the required ocular inspection
could be promptly undertaken as well.
- Be guided of the pertinent sections in the afore-mentioned regulations and in
other GPPB issued resolutions simplifying the requirements in project
implementation during emergency cases.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer shall stamp “PAID” all DVs
and its supporting documents after payment was made, to prevent the re-use of
the same on similar transaction.

Recommendations:
We hereby recommended to management the following:
- The Municipal Accountant ensures that the required supporting documents
of all claims are complete before affixing her/his signature in Box A of the
Disbursement Voucher.
- The management shall likewise observe that proper flow of transactions is
strictly followed so that only valid and proper claims are processed and
paid.

Recommendations:
We hereby recommended the following:
- The management shall prioritize finalization of the Municipality’s Solid
Waste Management Plan and include in the plan the required adjustments,
and so submission of the same to the National Solid Waste Commission for
approval could be undertaken.
- Ensure that implementation of the related solid waste management
programs/projects are authorized programs and projects included in the
approved plan, as any deviation from the said plan may pose doubts in the
said implementation and could be a ground for disallowance in audit.
- Complete other required documentation/data on Solid Waste Management
and immediately submit copy of the same to COA including the creation of
the Solid Waste Management Board and proof in the establishment of the
required solid waste facilities.
- Fully comply with the Solid Waste Management Act of 2000 and other
regulations pertaining thereto to be able to have a systematic and
comprehensive ecological waste management program.

observations.recommendation:
- The Bids and Awards Committee (BAC), BAC Secretariat and the agency’s
Technical Working Group (TWG) shall adhere to R.A 9184 and to other
procurement rules and regulations in all their procurement undertakings.
- Submit all lacking documentation to enable us complete our audit on the said
fund and further undertake the required ocular inspection. Moreover,
refrain from processing claims/payments not duly supported with complete
documentation so as to avoid audit suspension and disallowance.
- Observe deadlines in reports submission for a timely audit and inspection,
and immediate inclusion of its results in the Annual Audit report.

- The Bids and Awards Committee (BAC), BAC Secretariat and the agency’s
Technical Working Group (TWG) shall adhere to R.A 9184 and to other
procurement rules and regulations in all their procurement undertakings.
- The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer shall discontinue
processing of claims/payments for LDRRM related projects and activities
without appropriate documentation so as to avoid audit suspension and
disallowance.
- Concerned officials shall submit all lacking documentation to enable us
complete our audit on the said fund.

- Fast track finalization of the Solid Waste Management (SWM) Plan and have
it approved by the National Solid Waste Management Commission to ensure
that programs, projects and activities included in the plan are in accordance
with the National Solid Waste Management Framework.
- Complete other required documentation/data on Solid Waste Management
and submit copy to COA immediately afterwards.
- Ensure that proper mechanisms, like budget and manpower, are in place to
give assurance to proper implementation of the plan.
- Solid waste management programs/projects must align with the approved
SWM Plan. Any deviation from the said plan could be a ground for
disallowance in audit.
- Fully comply with the Solid Waste Management Act of 2000 and other
regulations pertaining thereto to be able to have a systematic and
comprehensive ecological waste management program.

- The management shall always formulate GAD Plan with great emphasis
on proper planning and compliance to various GAD policies and
regulations.
- Maximize budgeted amount for GAD to be able to meet its minimum
requirement.
- The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer shall discontinue
processing of claims/payments for GAD related projects and activities
without appropriate documentation so as to avoid audit suspension and
disallowance.
- Concerned officials shall submit all lacking documentation to enable us
complete our audit on the said fund.
- Annual Investment Plan (AIP) shall be prepared detailing therein all
programs, projects and activities to be implemented within the year
including PPAs for GAD Fund.

- The Municipal Treasurer shall coordinate with the Municipal Assessor in
the preparation of the required Certified List of Taxpayers and immediately
provide copy of the same to the Municipal Accountant to be able to establish
accurate amount of basic RPT and SET receivables.
- Upon receipt of the Latest Certified List of Taxpayers, the Municipal
Accountant shall make necessary adjusting entries to correct the recorded
amount of RPT/SET Receivable vis a vis the submitted list of taxpayers.
- The accounting office shall likewise maintain an index card for each
taxpayer to facilitate the establishment of the accurate amount of
collectibles from each taxpayer.
- Regularly update RPT/SET Receivable accounts Subsidiary Ledgers.

- Adhere with the afore-mentioned regulation on the releases of barangay
shares which is within five (5) days after the end of each quarter.
- Provide us copy of the updated RPT Income and Due to LGUs Subsidiary
Ledgers to enable us determine total RPT collections and total amount due
for remittance to respective LGUs.

- The Municipal Treasurer through the Municipal Accountant shall submit
to COA all checks that has been reported as cancelled.
- Ensure that all Report of Checks Issued shall bear copy of cancelled
checks (if there is any) to comply with the above-mentioned regulation.
However, if these checks could no longer be found despite diligent search
and effort, these have to be immediately reported to Head of Agency and
to COA, and likewise, submit the afore-mentioned required reports to
COA immediately thereafter.

We hereby recommended that management shall instruct concerned official to
submit immediately all the required documentation relative to its successful
implementation together with the breakdown of its actual expenditures and
other necessary data, to facilitate the conduct of our post-audit activity.

- Discontinue payment of claims not supported with complete documentations.
- Strictly observe correct disbursement system.
- Exert all possible efforts to settle all audit suspensions within the required
timeframe to avoid disallowance in audit.
- Assign additional qualified personnel to take charge in the settlement of all
audit suspensions to be able to meet the required deadlines.

- Head of Agency shall request and/or demand in writing from the Municipal
Treasurer to immediately prepare monthly reports of their transactions
including the required Report of Collections and Deposits, and submit the
same together with other supporting documents to the Accounting Section
within the deadlines set by regulation.
- Head of the accounting section shall prioritize submission of transaction
documents and other financial reports to COA and maximize work
assignment among accounting personnel so as to prevent work overload and
meet deadlines on reports submission.
- Take heed to the prescribed timeframe on submission of accounts and
financial reports to avoid sanctions that may be imposed from violating
thereof as required under section 127 of P.D. 1445, which provides that:
Administrative disciplinary actions:
Subject to rules and regulations as may be approved by the president,
any unjustified failure by the public officer concerned to comply with
any requirement imposed in this Code shall constitute neglect of duty
and shall be a ground for administrative disciplinary action against
the said public officer who, with the degree of his guilt in accordance
with the civil service law. Repeated unjustified failure to comply with
the requirements impose in this Code shall be conclusive proof the
public officer concerned is notoriously undesirable.

- Concerned offices shall give extra effort and prepare individual PPMPs
correctly and within the deadline.
- All procurement requirements shall be included in the prepared PPMPs
including those needed in the construction/repair of agency’s projects, and in
various GAD and LDRRM related undertakings.
- PPMPs shall be prepared annually and submit the same to the Bids and
Awards Committee (BAC) immediately thereafter for consolidation into
Annual Procurement Plan (APP) using the prescribed format as required in
the above-mentioned law and regulations. This approved APP shall serve as
the control in any agency’s procurement; hence, no procurement shall be
undertaken unless it is in accordance with the said plan.

We hereby recommended that Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) shall
undertake the following:
- Comply with the provisions stated in the procurement law specifically on
section 13.1 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184 , to enhance transparency in the
procurement process as well as make every procurement a valid
procurement.
- Take note of the five (5) day requirement wherein invitation should have been
sent to observers.
- Invite two (2) observers in addition to COA representation of known
qualifications and possesses the required expertise relevant to
goods/services being procured.

The management shall undertake the following:
- Submit LDRRM disbursement vouchers with complete and appropriate
supporting documents as well as settle audit suspensions within the
required timeframe to facilitate completion of our audit on said fund.
- The Municipal Budget Officer shall prepare and maintain separate
LDRRM Registry of Appropriation, Allotments and Obligations for
QRF, MOOE and Capital Outlay as required in the afore-mentioned
regulation.
- The Municipal Accountant shall also prepare and maintain subsidiary
records for LDRRM, and make it available to COA for the necessary
review and verification; and provide adequate disclosure in the Notes to
Financial Statements.
- Deposit the remaining unexpended balance from LDRRM MOOE and
Capital Outlay and provide us copy of the said fund transfer immediately
thereafter.

Municipal Budget Officer shall always takes heed to the requirement of the law
and ensure that budget records were updated regularly to be able to provide
reliable information towards fair presentation of agency’s Financial
Statements.

- Prepare the required AAPSI within the deadline set, which is within 60 days
upon receipt of the Annual Audit Report, following the correct format and
incorporating therein all audit findings and recommendations stated in Part
II of the said report.
- Submit immediately the duly accomplished AAPSI to enable us validates its
contents and prepare corresponding report as well.
- Provide copy of the said AAPSI to DBM, Speaker of the House of
Representatives, House Committee on Appropriations and Senate Committee
on Finance, as mandated in section 99 of R.A. 11260.
- Posting of the said report in the agency’s website is likewise enjoined.

Review and adopt systematic procedures in the processing of claims/disbursements that will provide a check and balance of functions and accountabilities.

Assigned qualified and efficient staff to assist the present workforce in accounting unit and in other agency’s key offices so that reports can be prepared promptly and within deadlines.

Require payees to submit the required documentation before payment is made.

Comply with the prescribed timeframe on the submission of accounts and financial reports to avoid sanctions that may be imposed for violating thereof as required under section 127 of P.D. 1445.
